acceptance_criteria: Pester unit tests pass on Windows + Linux.
Loader is order-stable (alphabetical) and recursive over `Private/`.
prd_refs: §8.2 Module manifest requirements — full keys, tags, project URI placeholders.
§9.2 acceptance — no config write during import.
description: Create the PowerShell module manifest and root script that the rest of the codebase plugs into. The `.psm1` loader must dot-source every `.ps1` under `Public/` and `Private/**/` deterministically, export only public functions, and never write to disk during `Import-Module` (per PRD §9.2 acceptance "no config write may occur during Import-Module").
notes: `AliasesToExport` for `/`, `//`, `/x`, `/!`, `/?` is set in `pub-01` after `ux-07` confirms which aliases are reliably installable.
GUID must be stable across versions — generate once and never regenerate.
steps: TDD: `Import-Module ./src/SlashOps/SlashOps.psd1` succeeds without error on Windows, macOS, Linux.
TDD: After import, no files exist under `~/.slashops/`.
TDD: The loader dot-sources files in stable alphabetical order (smoke test: add a probe file in `Private/` and assert it is loaded).

Author `SlashOps.psd1` with: `RootModule = 'SlashOps.psm1'`, `ModuleVersion = '0.1.0'`, generated stable `GUID`, `Author = 'Andre Sharpe'`, `Description` from PRD §8.2, `PowerShellVersion = '7.0'`, `CompatiblePSEditions = @('Core')`. Leave `FunctionsToExport = @()` for now; `pub-01` finalises it.
Add `.gitkeep` to `Public/` and `Private/` so the empty folders persist under git.
Write `tests/Unit/Module.Tests.ps1` covering the test list above.
name: [f-02] PowerShell module skeleton (psd1 + psm1 loader)
